introSection: The Rocket is a steel roller coaster currently located at Adventure Coast Southport in Southport, Merseyside, England, UK. The ride was one of four known Mini Mega Coaster MM29 units produced, one of Pinfari's last designs before going out of business.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The coaster first opened in 2003 at Codona's Amusement Park in Aberdeen, Scotland. It operated until 2014, and was subsequently transported to Gulliver's Warrington. It reopened the following year. The spot where it once stood at Codona's is now occupied by the Dead Man's Drop Tower and a children's play park. At Gulliver's, the Crazy Train operated until an incident on 15 September 2018, after which it remained closed and was eventually dismantled. It was sent to Southport Pleasureland where it opened in 2021.

Rocket isn't thought to have operated since an incident on 27 August 2022.

sections.title: Theme
sections.content:
At Codona's and Gulliver's, Crazy Train was painted purple with yellow supports. Prior to opening at Southport Pleasureland, it was repainted red with grey supports.

Unlike other Mini Mega Coaster MM29 roller coasters, the Crazy Train has a minecart themed train.

sections.title: Incidents
sections.content:
On 15 September 2018, a wheel assembly became detached, causing the train to stall. No injuries were sustained and all passengers were evacuated by fire crews within approximately two hours. The ride did not operate following the incident and was ultimately dismantled the following year. Another Mini Mega Coaster, Python at Gulliver's Land, was also closed following the incident.

On 27 August 2022, the train stopped suddenly part-way through the ride. Riders had to be rescued using a cherry picker. A child sustained a head injury and was knocked unconscious. She was treated at the scene before being taken to hospital.

fullText:
The Rocket is a steel roller coaster currently located at Adventure Coast Southport in Southport, Merseyside, England, UK. The ride was one of four known Mini Mega Coaster MM29 units produced, one of Pinfari's last designs before going out of business.

The coaster first opened in 2003 at Codona's Amusement Park in Aberdeen, Scotland. It operated until 2014, and was subsequently transported to Gulliver's Warrington. It reopened the following year. The spot where it once stood at Codona's is now occupied by the Dead Man's Drop Tower and a children's play park. At Gulliver's, the Crazy Train operated until an incident on 15 September 2018, after which it remained closed and was eventually dismantled. It was sent to Southport Pleasureland where it opened in 2021.

Rocket isn't thought to have operated since an incident on 27 August 2022.

At Codona's and Gulliver's, Crazy Train was painted purple with yellow supports. Prior to opening at Southport Pleasureland, it was repainted red with grey supports.

Unlike other Mini Mega Coaster MM29 roller coasters, the Crazy Train has a minecart themed train.

On 15 September 2018, a wheel assembly became detached, causing the train to stall. No injuries were sustained and all passengers were evacuated by fire crews within approximately two hours. The ride did not operate following the incident and was ultimately dismantled the following year. Another Mini Mega Coaster, Python at Gulliver's Land, was also closed following the incident.

On 27 August 2022, the train stopped suddenly part-way through the ride. Riders had to be rescued using a cherry picker. A child sustained a head injury and was knocked unconscious. She was treated at the scene before being taken to hospital.
